name: Celanova and Allariz Biosphere Reserve Loop
name_original: Celanova – vuelta del Reserva de Biosfera Área de Allariz
description: Cycle 33.3 miles (53.6 km) on this difficult touring route from Celanova through the scenic Allariz Biosphere Reserve.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Celanova offers varied terrain, from river valleys to hardwood forests and hills, providing diverse routes for cyclists. The region's undulating topography features both challenging climbs and exhilarating descents. Cyclists can explore areas with dense forests of birch, oak, and chestnut, particularly along riverbanks like the Arnoia. This combination of natural features makes Celanova a destination for outdoor activities. Best touring cycling routes around Celanova The most popular touring cycling route is Celanova - return of the Biosphere Reserve Area of Allariz, a 33.3 miles (53.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied landscapes, including river valleys and hardwood forests. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Outariz Hot Springs – Roman Bridge of Ourense loop from Celanova, a difficult 51.9 miles (83.5 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the River Arnoia and passes by historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Hórreos da Merca – Alto do San Marcos Viewpoint loop from A Merca, a 24.1 miles (38.9 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Celanova is defined by the River Arnoia, hardwood forests, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Celanova are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from 6 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Celanova's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Celanova?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Celanova, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes vary in distance and elevation, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Celanova?
A: The touring cycling routes in Celanova feature varied terrain, from lush river valleys like the Arnoia to dense hardwood forests of birch, oak, and chestnut. You'll also encounter undulating hills, providing both challenging climbs and exhilarating descents with scenic views. While many routes are well-paved, some segments may be unpaved.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Celanova?
A: While many routes in Celanova are considered moderate to difficult, there are options that can be enjoyed by those with a reasonable level of fitness. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hórreos da Merca loop from ENIL Voutureira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route, covering about 14.5 km with less elevation gain, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Celanova area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Celanova offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24552323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Roman Bridge of Ourense loop from Celanova&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 83.5 km path with significant elevation. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017200936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celanova - return of the Biosphere Reserve Area of Allariz&lt;/a&gt;, which is 53.6 km long and traverses varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural attractions like waterfalls or springs?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to beautiful natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction in the area. Additionally, the region features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vieiro Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the natural landscape often encountered on rides.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or cultural landmarks while cycling in Celanova?
A: Absolutely. Celanova and its surroundings are rich in history and culture. You can cycle past sites like the magnificent 10th-century Monasterio de Salvador in Celanova itself, or visit the well-preserved late Middle Age tower in Vilanova dos Infantes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3414481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreos of A Merca&lt;/a&gt; are also a unique cultural landmark you might encounter on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hórreos da Merca – Alto do San Marcos Viewpoint loop from A Merca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the River Arnoia is a prominent natural feature in the region, and many cycling routes incorporate its scenic banks. The San Rosendo Nature Trail, for instance, offers a tranquil experience along the left bank of the River Arnoia, guiding cyclists through lush riparian vegetation and past traditional Galician mills.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Celanova?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Celanova are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24552323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Roman Bridge of Ourense loop from Celanova&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereiras Fountain loop from Taboadela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 39.6 km route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Celanova?
A: The touring cycling routes in Celanova are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from 6 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied landscapes, from river valleys to hardwood forests, and the combination of challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife or enjoy nature on the routes?
A: The region's diverse natural environment, including hardwood forests of birch, oak, and chestnut, and river valleys like the Arnoia and Regato de Santa Olaia, provides ample opportunities to observe local wildlife and enjoy nature. Cyclists often pass through areas with dense vegetation, offering a serene outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any hot springs near the cycling routes for relaxation?
A: Yes, after a day of cycling, you can find opportunities for relaxation at nearby hot springs. Attractions like Caldas de Outariz and Termas Prexigueiro are within reach, offering a perfect way to unwind and recover.
tips: Celanova's main square has bars and restaurants and is a great place to start any adventure. Like many towns in the region, its origin is pre-Roman and it is located on the trade route between Portugal and Ourense. The most outstanding places are the Mosteiro de Celanova and the Chapel of San Miguel de Celanova.
A small rural church in the town of Corvillón.
Allariz is a small historical town strategically located on the Arnoia river. Famous for the leather trade and tanning that used river water in the production process.
Walk on both sides of the river ideal for cycling and walking, very cool in summer with areas where you can have a drink
Well preserved grain storage. The wooden structure raised on granite stone pads was used to store locally grown grain and corn away from rain and rodents.
landscape in the river Arnoia, really cinema

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain, including river plains, mixed woodlands, cultivated areas, and oak forests. You'll often follow the winding Arnoia River valley with rolling hills. While many sections are paved, be prepared for some unpaved segments and significant elevation changes, which contribute to the route's challenging nature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 53.6 km long with an elevation gain of about 814 meters. It typically takes around 3 hours and 38 minutes to complete, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is best suited for experienced touring cyclists with very good fitness. It features substantial elevation gains and can include challenging unpaved sections, which might even require dismounting and pushing your bike in some parts.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You'll pass through picturesque landscapes and historic sites. Notable highlights include the Main Square of Celanova, the Church of Santa María de Corvillón, the historic town of Allariz, the Riverside Walk Along the Arnoia River – Allariz, the View of Hórreos da Merca, and the Freixo Roman Bridge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, considering the varied terrain and potential for unpaved sections, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter weather, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Allariz Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve is a UNESCO-recognized area, but typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the general reserve area itself. However, always check local regulations for any specific protected zones or private properties you might encounter.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs on touring bicycle routes within the Allariz Biosphere Reserve are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife, and to ensure they are fit enough for the route's difficult rating and distance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and returns to Celanova. You should be able to find parking within Celanova, particularly near the town center or other public parking areas, to begin your touring bicycle adventure.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns, most notably Allariz and Celanova. Both towns offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and refresh during your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camiño Natural de San Rosendo, Camiño Xabrés por Verín, and Mtb Camiño Xabrés por Verín, among others.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: The name 'Celanova - return of the Biosphere Reserve Area of Allariz' indicates that the route starts and finishes in Celanova, suggesting it is a loop or a circuit that brings you back to your starting point.
